perturbation.changed_text:
Sponsor will make payments to Excite according to the following schedule:
DATE PAYMENT
----
-------
Effective Date [****]
Commencement Date [****]
12/31/97 [****]
1/1/98 [****]
4/1/98 [****]
7/1/98 [****]
10/1/98 [****]
1/1/99 [****]
4/1/99 [****]
7/1/98 [****]
Sponsor shall have the option to defer any payment listed above by up to 90 days, provided that Sponsor notifies Excite in writing at least 10 days before the original due date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a clause that allows Sponsor to defer payments. This contradicts the earlier, rigid payment schedule, creating uncertainty about when Excite can expect payment. This introduces a conflict between the original, specified payment dates and the newly added option for the sponsor to delay them by 90 days. The added clause creates uncertainty as to when the payment is expected to be made.

Sponsor shall have the option to defer any payment listed above by up to 90 days, provided that Sponsor notifies Excite in writing at least 10 days before the original due date.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a fixed payment schedule. The changed text introduces a clause allowing the Sponsor to defer payments by up to 90 days, contradicting the fixed schedule and creating uncertainty about payment dates, leading to an in-text contradiction per the definition of inconsistencies.
